mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth rate is positive for two consecutive quarters, yet S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer confidence, leading to reduced spending and lower corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.2% (t-1mo) indicates a weak labor market
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE rises by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low, but inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, it signals potential stagflation or a delayed monetary policy response, leading to investor uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth indicates strong manufacturing and supply-side activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon, especially during recovery phases.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth during recovery, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate healthy consumer spending and confidence, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a strong and expanding economy, leading to increased consumer demand and corporate profits, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are declining while the Federal Funds Rate is already very low, it signals a deeper structural weakness in the economy that monetary policy is failing to address, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where HOUST is declining significantly and FEDFUNDS is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extreme monetary accommodation (very low FEDFUNDS), the market often anticipates future economic weakness or policy tightening, leading to uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns as investors de-risk.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive (more than 60% of instances) when FEDFUNDS is below 0.15%.
